title: Data Entry Specialist
description:
Description

Our Services team have unrivalled expertise working within the corporate governance, audit, risk, compliance, and regulatory environment in a wide variety of sectors. We have the scale and reach to deliver significant change, smoothly and without disruption. Our experience in solution design and delivery helps us provide global, dynamic, flexible, and reliable distribution, giving clients lasting results. Our vision is to match client requirements by partnering with them and becoming a trusted advisor to their evolving business. We work alongside them in joint teams, complementing their strengths and establishing teams with the right mix of talent.

The Services team is also an instrumental part of innovation within Diligent, collaborating with other departments to bring new value to the product in the form of new or improved solutions.

We are looking for a highly professional, innovative, and technically motivated person to perform Data Specialist duties as part of the Services team.

Key Responsibilities



Accurately and efficiently enter client corporate governance data into a designated spreadsheet or Diligent Entities system.
Execute data migration activities to move client books from Diligent Boards site to SFS or other Boards site.
Develop Director & Officer questionnaires in Diligent Boards Questionnaire module.
Migration of entity documents to data library in Diligent Entities system.
Ensure the data is correctly formatted according to the specified guidelines, including appropriate columns, rows, headers, and formatting styles.
Conduct regular quality checks to verify the accuracy and integrity of the entered data, identifying and correcting any errors or inconsistencies.
Validate the entered data against the source documents or information to ensure the information is entered correctly and matches the original records.
Maintain strict confidentiality and data security protocols to protect sensitive client information and adhere to data protection regulations.
Complete data entry tasks within the specified deadlines and prioritise work effectively to meet project requirements and client expectations.
Maintain an organised system for managing and tracking data entry tasks, including proper documentation of completed work and any relevant notes or observations.
Collaborate with team members or clients to clarify any data-related questions, seek clarification on unclear instructions, and provide regular updates on the progress of assigned tasks.
Identify and resolve any technical issues or challenges related to data entry systems, software, or tools, or escalate them to the appropriate technical support team.
Categorising, uploading, and downloading bulk documents to and from Diligent Software.
Suggest and implement process improvements to enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and effectiveness of data entry tasks, such as developing templates, automation, or standardising procedures.
Flexibly handle changes in project requirements, data formats, or systems, and quickly adapt to new processes or tools to meet evolving needs.
Good Understanding of Database is must.
SQL intermediate level skill set is desired.
AI and AI tools exposure is required.
Any other duties as may reasonably be required.



Required Experience/Skills:



Proven experience interacting directly with client stakeholders at all levels
Previous experience in a similar role
Self-directed, proactive, strong problem solving/troubleshooting skills
Ability to work as part of a team in a multi-discipline fast-paced environment
Strong attention to detail to ensure the accuracy and integrity of entered data, including identifying and correcting errors or inconsistencies.
Excellent communication skills (both verbal and written) and interpersonal skills, able to communicate with a wide range of stakeholders (e.g., both technical and business)
Excellent personal time management and ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment
Proactive attitude toward suggesting and implementing process improvements, automation, or streamlining data entry tasks to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
Ability to utilise advanced Excel sheet functions
Excel scripting (VBA) Desirable
Experience / understanding of Corporate Governance Desirable



Customer Delivery



Perform data migration activities including data entry, data imports, questionnaire development and other related tasks.



Career Development



Proactively focus on developing Product and Domain knowledge and expertise through continuous learning and personal development.
Learn and apply relevant programming language(s) such as SQL, ACL, Python, and any other relevant tools needed to be successful and efficient in the role.



About Us

Diligent is the AI leader in governance, risk and compliance (GRC) SaaS solutions, helping more than 1 million users and 700,000 board members to clarify risk and elevate governance. The Diligent One Platform gives practitioners, the C-Suite and the board a consolidated view of their entire GRC practice so they can more effectively manage risk, build greater resilience and make better decisions, faster.

At Diligent, we're building the future with people who think boldly and move fast. Whether you're designing systems that leverage large language models or part of a team reimaging workflows with AI, you'll help us unlock entirely new ways of working and thinking. Curiosity is in our DNA, we look for individuals willing to ask the big questions and experiment fearlessly - those who embrace change not as a challenge, but as an opportunity. The future belongs to those who keep learning, and we are building it together. At Diligent, you’re not just building the future - you’re an agent of positive change, joining a global community on a mission to make an impact.

Learn more at diligent.com or follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook

What Diligent Offers You



Creativity is ingrained in our culture. We are innovative collaborators by nature. We thrive in exploring how things can be differently both in our internal processes and to help our clients
We care about our people. Diligent offers a flexible work environment, global days of service, comprehensive health benefits, meeting free days, generous time off policy and wellness programs to name a few
We have teams all over the world. We may be headquartered in New York City, but we have office hubs in Washington D.C., Vancouver, London, Galway, Budapest, Munich, Bengaluru, Singapore, and Sydney.
Diversity is important to us. Growing, maintaining and promoting a diverse team is a top priority for us. We foster and encourage diversity through our Employee Resource Groups and provide access to resources and education to support the education of our team, facilitate dialogue, and foster understanding.



Diligent created the modern governance movement. Our world-changing idea is to empower leaders with the technology, insights and connections they need to drive greater impact and accountability – to lead with purpose. Our employees are passionate, smart, and creative people who not only want to help build the software company of the future, but who want to make the world a more sustainable, equitable and better place.

Headquartered in New York, Diligent has offices in Washington D.C., London, Galway, Budapest, Vancouver, Bengaluru, Munich, Singapore and Sydney.  To foster strong collaboration and connection, this role will follow a hybrid work model. If you are within a commuting distance to one of our Diligent office locations, you will be expected to work onsite at least 50% of the time. We believe that in-person engagement helps drive innovation, teamwork, and a strong sense of community.

We are a drug free workplace. Diligent is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religious creed, sex, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, pregnancy, childbirth, physical disability, mental disability, age, military status, protected veteran status, marital status, registered domestic partner or civil union status, gender (including sex stereotyping and gender identity or expression), medical condition (including, but not limited to, cancer related or HIV/AIDS related), genetic information, or sexual orientation in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Diligent's EEO Policy and Know Your Rights. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, you may contact us at recruitment@diligent.com.

To all recruitment agencies: Diligent does not accept unsolicited agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, Diligent employees or any other organization location. Diligent is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

title: Supply Chain Associate Manager
description:
About This Role

Wells Fargo is seeking a Supply Chain Associate Manager

In This Role, You Will


Supervise entry-to-mid level roles in planning and implementing purchasing programs, on-going measurement, monitoring, and reporting of supplier performance tasks and processes to ensure timely completion, quality and compliance in Supply Chain
Identify opportunities for all aspects of negotiations and may customize legal language and draft non-standard contract terms and conditions to proactively address supply chain and business issues in less complex functions
Make day-to-day supervisory and tactical decisions and resolve issues related to team supervision, work allocation and daily operations under direction of Supply Chain management
Leverage interpretation of policies, procedures, and compliance requirements
Collaborate and consult with Vendors, and managers
Train less experienced supply chain managers within the team
Manage allocation of people and financial resources for Supply Chain
Be responsible for selection, evaluation, and development of staff


Required Qualifications:


2+ years of Supply Chain experience or equivalent demonstrated through one or a combination of the following: work experience, training, military experience, education
1+ years of leadership experience


Desired Qualifications:


Experience in Supply Chain, or any Risk Management field involving identifying, assessing, mitigating and monitoring of Risks, experience in Onboarding, Contracting, Monitoring and Termination would also be a required skill
People Management experience
Ability to analyze and understand Process Gaps along with the impact and consequence if realized that the process is not in alignment with the required policies.
GDPR, CIPM, FIPS OR any risk Management certification
CISA OR CRISC OR Certification on vendor risk Management
Develop and report Key Risk indicators for the business as FLOD


Job Expectations:


Leverage interpretation of policies, procedures, and compliance requirements
Collaborate and consult with Vendors, and managers
Manage and develop a highly skilled team of supply chain professionals, to align and execute Third Party Program Policies and Procedures
Under the Third-Party Risk program and operating model of Lines of Business and the bank itself for a flawless execution
Strong understanding of Risk elements like Identifications, Assessing, Mitigation and monitoring of Risks
Strong understanding of how each of the risks pertaining to Reputations, Background checks, BCP, information security and Data elements are assessed and mitigated
Should possess a strong understanding of the international regulations like GDPR, CPRA, GLBA, PDPA, FIPS, also relating to information security and Data privacy etc
Should be able to articulate the Risk impact and cull out a mitigation plan to manage the risk identified
Drive compliance within the process in line with the third Party Risk Management policies
Collaborate and work with the Enterprise Partners to understand changes and updates to the regulations and guide the team in delivering as per the updates and requirements
Identify Risk proof innovation and process improvement opportunities within the influence of regulations.
Demonstrate and drive the required Governance in order to ensure there is alignment to the policies and procedures
Collaborate with the Lines of Business and support in driving change and help them adapt with the updates and changes received through regulations, rules and guidelines
Mentor and guide talent development of direct reports and assist in hiring talent
Should possess experience in Managing Asst Manager and Senior Risk professional and help them the companywide objective and goals
Conduct team meetings, appraisals and regular/ skip levels connects to drive a better work culture
Understand the Code of Ethics and Integrity of Wells Fargo
Understand the Wells Fargo Supply Chain Dynamics and landscape.
Get a good hold on Third Party Risk Management Processes
Understand the Risk Management Process especially the TPRM nuances.
Ability to understand the process and drive process improvement.
Manage the team and provide them with adequate and timely feedback.
Perform Mid-Year and Year end appraisals.
Build a High performing team and driving Flawless execution.
Be an advocacy of Change and innovation


Posting End Date:

3 Apr 2026


Job posting may come down early due to volume of applicants.


We Value Equal Opportunity

Wells Fargo is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Employees support our focus on building strong customer relationships balanced with a strong risk mitigating and compliance-driven culture which firmly establishes those disciplines as critical to the success of our customers and company. They are accountable for execution of all applicable risk programs (Credit, Market, Financial Crimes, Operational, Regulatory Compliance), which includes effectively following and adhering to applicable Wells Fargo policies and procedures, appropriately fulfilling risk and compliance obligations, timely and effective escalation and remediation of issues, and making sound risk decisions. There is emphasis on proactive monitoring, governance, risk identification and escalation, as well as making sound risk decisions commensurate with the business unit's risk appetite and all risk and compliance program requirements.

Candidates applying to job openings posted in Canada: Applications for employment are encouraged from all qualified candidates, including women, persons with disabilities, aboriginal peoples and visible minorities. title: Graduate
description:
Overview

WELCOME TO SITA

We're the team that keeps airports moving, airlines flying smoothly, and borders open. Our tech and communication breakthroughs are the foundation of the global air travel industry.

You'll find us at 95% of international hubs. We partner closely with over 2,500 transportation and government clients, each with their own unique needs and challenges. Our goal is to find fresh solutions and cutting-edge tech to make their operations run like clockwork. Want to be a part of something big?

Are you ready to love your job? The adventure begins right here, with you, at SITA.

About The Role & Team

SITA India is on the lookout for recent graduates to join our SkyStar Program within the Procurement function, initially for a 12-month period, with the potential for a permanent role.

Role

Your main tasks will involve supporting the manufacturing procurement and the sourcing specialists and provide supporting data and MI to create more informed procurements as well as play a hands-on role in direct sourcing activity, obtaining quotes, supporting RFx`s and E Sourcing initiatives, providing assistance under supervision of the Manufacturing Procurement lead, and ensuring the smooth operation of procurement. This entry-level opportunity offers a chance to dive into various activities within our fast-paced global community:


Connecting with Diverse Individuals: Collaborate locally and globally.
Building Skills for Your Career: Develop essential skills tailored to your job stream.
Joining Corporate Projects: Contribute to projects shaping the future of our industry.


Early career talent is our future, and we're thrilled to have you join impactful missions alongside our experts, gaining valuable experiences. We can't wait to witness the incredible things you'll accomplish with us!

Empowered People, Reinventing Travel

What You’ll Do


Support Operative Project Procurement by assisting in day‑to‑day purchasing activities for ongoing projects, including data preparation, order follow‑up, supplier coordination, and documentation management
Collaborate with project managers and engineering teams to ensure materials and services are procured in line with project timelines, specifications, and quality requirements.
Follow up with suppliers on project‑critical deliveries, order confirmations, documentation requests, and issue resolution to support smooth project execution.
Support procurement transformation initiatives, contributing to process improvements, digital tools adoption, standardization, and increased operational efficiency within the Procurement team.
Ensure compliance with internal procurement policies, documentation standards, and approval workflows while maintaining accurate records.


Qualifications

KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS


Strong analytical skills with attention to detail
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
Ability to learn quickly and work in a dynamic team environment
High level of motivation, curiosity, and willingness to take initiative
Basic understanding of procurement, supply chain, or manufacturing processes
Knowledge of e‑sourcing tools or ERP platforms


Education & Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in supply chain management, Business Administration, Engineering, Commerce, or a related field.


What We Offer

We're all about diversity. We operate in 200 countries and speak 60 different languages and cultures. We're really proud of our inclusive environment. Our offices are comfortable and fun places to work, and we make sure you get to work from home too. Find out what it's like to join our team and take a step closer to your best life ever.

🏡 Flex Week: Work from home up to 2 days/week (depending on your team's needs)

⏰ Flex Day: Make your workday suit your life and plans.

🌎 Flex-Location: Take up to 30 days a year to work from any location in the world.

🌿 Employee Wellbeing: We have got you covered with our Employee Assistance Program (EAP), for you and your dependents 24/7, 365 days/year. We also offer Champion Health - a personalized platform that supports a range of wellbeing needs.

🚀 Professional Development: At SITA, we believe growth fuels innovation. Our learning ecosystem offers access to world-class platforms and programs designed to help you thrive. From LinkedIn Learning, Microsoft's Enterprise Skills Initiative, and Airport Council International -available to all employees-to specialized solutions like Pluralsight for technology upskilling, Harvard Business Publishing for people leadership, Stanford for strategic development and many others, we align learning opportunities with your Development Plan and our business priorities. Your development journey is supported every step of the way.

🙌 Competitive Benefits: Competitive benefits that make sense with both your local market and employment status.

SITA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value a diverse workforce. In support of our Employment Equity Program, we encourage women, aboriginal people, members of visible minorities, and/or persons with disabilities to apply and self-identify in the application process.

title: Technical Support Specialist
description:
The ideal candidate will be responsible for configuring and troubleshooting our product to resolve our customers' technical issues. You will support the customer by acting as the liaison between the customer and other internal teams. Your ability to work in complex networking environment will also make you an ideal candidate.




Looking for immediate joiners only.




Responsibilities




Ability to work in 24*7 environment.
Excellent communication skills (International Voice process) is non-negotiable.
Responsible for responding to customer requests, including but not limited to, answering calls via a phone queue, customer emails, chat support requests and providing after hours on call support for troubleshooting technical issues.
Support end-user devices/peripherals, including but not limited to computer hardware, operating systems, communications, software applications, data processing and security.
Support activities include supporting end-users accepting IT break-fix requests from customers across multiple entry points, providing IT break/fix issue resolution or escalation of the issue to the appropriate team.
First line help desk; resolve basic level 1 and some level 2 issues including remotely troubleshooting issues relating to hardware and software (typically Windows, Mac OS, Apple, Google for Work, Android Phone Support, MDM, AirWatch etc.)
Exposure to retail industry devices such as Point-of-Sale devices, Zebra devices, switches, routers, printers, other store equipment devices is preferred.
Perform post-resolution follow-ups to help requests or incidents.
Research issues and resolves technical problems.
Creating/updating knowledge articles and support processes functions




Qualifications

Experience – Min. 6 Months to 1 Year (Mandatory) and 2+ Years (Preferred)
Written and verbal communication skills.
Exceptional ability to multitask.
Exceptional ability to adapt and learn new platforms and skills on the fly.
Exceptional sense of urgency
Exceptional interpersonal skills, with a focus on rapport-building, listening, and questioning skills.
First Line Troubleshooting to be done completely using the available resources and documented before assigning/routing the tickets to the L2 Groups.
Hands on experience on ITSM tools like HPSM, BMC Remedy, Service Now, CA Service Desk, Monitoring Tools.
Good to have - Experience using and proficient in Apple hardware, Mac OS, Google (G-Suite), Windows and Office365
Good to have – ITIL Knowledge
Graduate from an accredited institution or Under-graduate with relevant technical experience
Previous customer/technical support or technology experience




Interested candidates can send your resumes to khushbu.ahuja@techmahindra.com

title: Production Assistant
description:
Eyeline is a global team of risk takers, innovators and perfectionists who achieve creative visions and have fun doing it. A dynamic workforce nestled within the industry’s most intriguing cities, united by a drive to push the boundaries of storytelling. Learn more.

In this entry-level role, you will provide administrative and general support to the production team to help them navigate a fast-paced and fast-shifting environment.

Key Responsibilities


Assists and supports the Producer, Production Manager, and Production Coordinators in day-to-day activities, general production errands, and tasks on assigned projects
Maintains crew lists in Shotgrid, helps update & maintain show email aliases
Acts as an assistant to the VFX Coordinator to help follow up on items after dailies
Shadow/ Support VFX Coordinators on artist rounds and serves as a backup point of contact for artist
Creates, updates, and maintains VFX show calendar including dailies, meetings, etc
Notifies crew of dailies time and location
Note-taking in meetings, and learning to input into appropriate communication channels (Shotgrid/ confluence)
Helps set up client calls, and inputs client notes
Helps those who need translation

Excellent communication skills in English
Film/CG/VFX experience including student/personal project (preferred)

Since our beginning in 1989, we strive to set the highest standard of quality in everything we do. We look for explorers, people who are willing to go to new places and are open to testing, learning and iterating as they go. Scanline VFX is an award-winning leader in the visual effects industry with 7 studios worldwide, including London, Los Angeles, Montreal, Seoul, Mumbai, Hyderabad and Vancouver. title: BSA - Assistant Manager - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Senior Business Systems Analyst (BSA) Location: Hyderabad, India Are you passionate about technology and interested in joining a community of collaborative colleagues who respectfully and courageously seek to challenge the status quo? If so, read on to learn more about an exciting opportunity with Deloitte’s Technology (DT). We are curious and life-long learners focused on technology and innovation. Work you’ll do


Responsible for leading conversations with stakeholders and translating technical discussions and directions into functional business requirements through detailed acceptance criterias.
Responsible for implementing organizational delivery standards, practitioner training, work scope management and the continuous enhancement of BA practitioner delivery capabilities.
Responsible for ensuring all business analysis and quality assurance work products adhere to the practices and standards defined by Community of Practice and project leaders.
Responsible for active participation in appropriate professional Community of Practice forums.
Responsible for determining level of effort estimations, perform task execution and meeting established project milestones.
Responsible for the timeliness and accuracy of necessary escalations, producing and maintaining applicable project metrics and status reporting within a project team.
Collaborates with technical delivery team members to maximize and leverage the understanding of business solution functional requirements and non-functional requirements.
Responsible for the documentation of formal requirements, use cases, user stories, test plans, test cases, test scripts and test suites which ensure that the software solutions developed and tested are fit for purpose, meet business needs and are operationally sustainable.
Responsible for complying with time reporting and other mandated organizational and corporate policies. Duties & Responsibilities:
Understands business strategy and objectives by studying the business function, gathering information, and evaluating output requirements and formats. Elicits and documents functional requirements and test strategies for technical solutions to meet business needs.
Possesses advanced level understanding of BA, QA/QC professional standards, business processes, workflows, methodologies and leading practices.
Ensure for traceability and optimum coverage of business requirements.
Collaborates with application developers, designers, and architects to ensure a shared understanding of the business model and the detailed function requirements.
Establishes the estimation of requirements documentation and testing efforts for various projects and initiatives.
Provides senior management and project management with regular updates on deliverable timeline and project status.
Responsible for conducting Design Thinking sessions to gain insights into end user and customer needs. Manages the Product Backlog for a product, working with the Technical Delivery Manager to prioritize requirements, define and manage scope and sprints for a project
Works with Technical Delivery Manager and end users to plan and execute User Acceptance Testing.
Participates in reviews of plans associated with project execution.
Responds proactively to both business and project issues and escalates appropriately.
Ensures communication of Change Management activities and confirms solution and schedule adaptability
Performs other job-related duties as assigned. Qualifications:
Minimum 7-8 years of work experience as a Business Analyst.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Business Administration or equivalent education. Required Technical Skills:
Strong relationship building and consulting skills. Fosters a climate conducive to establishing positive working relationships with clients and cross functional team members
Strong communication, technical and remote collaboration skills are a must
Advanced knowledge of Agile methodology
Excellent organization, analytical and planning skills
Experience in dealing with multiple projects and cross-functional teams and ability to coordinate across teams in a large matrix organization environment
Familiarity with SharePoint and automated QA tools is a plus
Mobile application design and testing experience is a plus
Understanding Java, HTML, Microsoft Office and test automation scripting methodologies is a plus
Knowledge of designing wireframe tools like Figma is a plus. Preferred:
Experience working with Agile/SAFe development processes
Experience in products like Azure Devops/ServiceNow etc.
ISTQB, CBAP or CCBA certifications are a plus
Scrum master certification is a plus. How you’ll grow At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to help them to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to help sharpen skills in addition to hands-on experience in the global, fast-changing business world. From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career. Benefits At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. We value our people and offer employees a broad range of benefits. Deloitte’s culture Our positive and supportive culture encourages our people to do their best work every day. We celebrate individuals by recognizing their uniqueness and offering them the flexibility to make daily choices that can help them to be healthy, centered, confident, and aware. We offer well-being programs and are continuously looking for new ways to maintain a culture where our people excel and lead healthy, happy lives. Corporate citizenship Deloitte is led by a purpose: to make an impact that matters. This purpose defines who we are and extends to relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. We believe that business has the power to inspire and transform. We focus on education, giving, skill-based volunteerism, and leadership to help drive positive social impact in our communities. Recruiter tips We want job seekers exploring opportunities at Deloitte to feel prepared and confident. To help you with your interview, we suggest that you do your research: know some background about the organization and the business area you’re applying to.


Our purpose

Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities.

Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.

Professional development

At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India .

Benefits To Help You Thrive

At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you.

Recruiting tips

From developing a stand out resume to putting your best foot forward in the interview, we want you to feel prepared and confident as you explore opportunities at Deloitte. Check out recruiting tips from Deloitte recruiters.

title: GTM Associate
description:
An accelerated Leadership Track for fresh graduates and recent graduates (0-2 years) who want to build, learn, and lead early: compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.




The Opportunity

Designed for an extremely limited set of exceptional fresh graduates stepping into their early full-time role, this position offers a non-linear, accelerated path to leadership. The Founder’s Office – GTM Associate is a high-intensity track built around real ownership, steep learning curves, and close collaboration with founders and senior leaders — compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.

You’ll work on high-impact initiatives across growth, strategy, customer engagement, and market expansion. Top performers quickly take on ownership, lead teams, and drive initiatives across geographies. This is not a conventional entry-level job. This is a launchpad for future business leaders.




What you'll do

Work directly with founders and senior leaders on GTM strategy, execution, and experimentation
Drive market expansion and international go-to-market initiatives
Own cross-functional projects across sales, marketing, customer success, partnerships, and product
Lead revenue growth and funnel optimization initiatives
Analyze markets, customers, and performance data to identify growth opportunities
Engage with global customers to uncover pain points and build customer-first solutions
Support and lead new launches, pilots, and strategic projects
Collaborate across geographies and functions to deliver outcomes
Take end-to-end ownership of initiatives and be accountable for results
Over time, you will move from: Execution → Ownership → Market/Function Leadership → Team Leadership / P&L Responsibility
Our expectation is clear: 




Within ~2 years, high performers will be operating at a team-lead level. This program is built for people who want accelerated responsibility, not gradual hand-holding.




Who we're looking for

We seek outliers and you might be a fit if you have:

Undergraduate degree in any discipline from a Tier 1 institution, completed by June 2026 (or final examinations completed by then). Open to recent graduates with 0–2 years of experience
Willingness to commit to a minimum of two years with the organization
Strong spoken and written English communication
High learning agility, curiosity, and initiative-taking ability
Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven mindset—you see problems as opportunities
Strong business aptitude, logical reasoning, and comfort with numbers and data
Structured, strategic thinking with exceptional attention to detail
Proven problem-solving skills and customer empathy
Passionate hustler mentality with the drive to execute, not just plan
Excited by ambiguity, high expectations, and real responsibility
Aspiration to become a future business leader, not just a functional specialist
Hunger to work on cutting-edge, industry-leading problems
Bonus: Proficiency in a foreign language




About DeltaX

Founded in 2012, DeltaX is an AI & Automation company driving transformation primarily in the area of Marketing & Advertising. Our flagship product is a data-driven digital advertising platform designed for agencies and advertisers to seamlessly plan, create, buy, track, attribute, and report campaigns across search, social, display RTB, mobile, video, and other digital channels. Its cutting-edge technology empowers marketing teams worldwide with deep insights, automated ROI-based spend optimisation, and the ability to execute award-winning, technology-driven campaigns. The scope of our offering has compounded in width and depth with AI and automation being at the forefront of it. 




Evaluation Process

Getting to Know You + Assignment Submission via Google Form
Interviews
Offer Roll-out

Mio Overview

T The Marketing Science Operations (MSO) vertical at Amgen is the operational backbone that ensures accurate, timely, governed, and analytics ready marketing data across all digital channels. MSO enables the MIO (Marketing Investment & Optimization), and broader Decision Sciences teams to run self-service analytics, generate insights, and execute campaigns reliably.

MSO focuses on data readiness, governance, pipeline operations, vendor accountability, and automation with the goal of delivering clean, standardized, and insight-ready datasets.

Role Summary

The Associate – MSO BI Reporting role is entry level analytics and reporting position within the Marketing Science Operations (MSO) BI team. This role is ideal for candidates who are interested in building strong foundations in business intelligence, reporting, and data quality.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an MSO BI Reporting Associate, you will work under the guidance of Senior Associates and Managers to support BI reporting, dashboarding, and data quality operations.

You will:


Support development and maintenance of standard BI dashboards and reports.
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and QA checks to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Assist in data extraction, transformation, and preparation for reporting use cases.
Monitor data refreshes, pipeline health, and reporting completeness.
Help document metric definitions, data sources, and reporting logic.
Support regular weekly and monthly reporting cycles.
Identify data issues, anomalies, or gaps and escalate appropriately; Closely collaborate with Data Engineering stakeholders.


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's (3 to 6 Yrs) or master's (2 to 4 Yrs) degree required in any discipline with strong record of academic success in quantitative and analytic coursework such as operations research, applied mathematics, management science, data science, statistics, econometrics, or engineering.
Experience in Tableau, Power BI, or similar visualization tools; Familiarity with dashboards, KPIs, or recurring reports
Effective oral and written communication skills.
Comfort working with structured data, tables, and reports.


Preferred Qualifications


Basic experience with Excel, SQL, or any BI/reporting tool
Interest in data governance, reporting standards, or analytics operations.
Experience supporting analytics in pharma, healthcare, or regulated environments.
Effective communication skills with the ability to collaborate closely with Senior Associates to deliver high‑quality analytical outputs.
Exposure to DevSecOps practice
Exposure to Incident Management

title: Associate Data Scientist (Commercial)
description:
Associate Data Scientist (L3) – Commercial (Gen Med, AIN)

HOW MIGHT YOU DEFY IMAGINATION?

If you feel like you’re part of something bigger, it’s because you are. At Amgen, our shared mission—to serve patients—drives all that we do. We are global collaborators who achieve together—researching, manufacturing, and delivering ever-better products that reach millions of patients worldwide. It’s time for a career you can be proud of.

Role Overview

The Associate Data Scientist is an entry-level role supporting data science initiatives across Amgen’s General Medicine (Gen Med) portfolio. This role will work closely with Data Science Leads and senior team members to contribute to analytics, modeling, and insight generation, while building strong foundations in applied data science.

Live | What you will do


Support analytics and data science projects across commercial use cases in the Gen Med portfolio
Assist in data preparation, exploratory analysis, and feature engineering to enable modeling efforts
Contribute to development and testing of predictive models and analytical frameworks under guidance of senior data scientists
Perform metric tracking, performance analysis, and validation checks with high attention to detail
Support creation of insight summaries, visualizations, and presentations for business stakeholders
Maintain and update code, dashboards, and analytical assets in Python/SQL environments
Follow best practices for documentation, QA, and reproducibility
Collaborate with team members to meet project timelines and communicate progress effectively
Engage in continuous learning across predictive modeling, experimentation, and commercial analytics methods


Thrive | What you can expect


Structured learning and hands-on experience in commercial data science applications
Exposure to end-to-end analytics delivery across brands and use cases
Mentorship from experienced data scientists and opportunities to grow into independent contributors
A collaborative environment focused on learning, impact, and development

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field
1–3 years of experience in analytics, data science, or related roles
Working knowledge of Python and SQL
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Attention to detail and ability to deliver high-quality, accurate work


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to predictive modeling, experimentation, or statistical analysis
Familiarity with commercial analytics or marketing performance measurement
Interest in healthcare, pharma, or life sciences analytics
Experience with tools such as Jupyter notebooks, Git, or data visualization platforms
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders
Strong communication skills and willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment

title: Process Associate- Fresher
description:
Job Title: Process Associate (Fresher)

Location: Hyderabad, Telangana

Company: Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd.

About Us

Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd. (HUMS) is a dynamic and innovative company dedicated to transforming the healthcare industry. We take pride in being a reliable Healthcare Management partner. With several years of experience, we have perfected our end-to-end services in medical billing, A.R. management, and other essential healthcare operations.

We provide services to Harmony United Psychiatric Care, a US-based Healthcare Company. Our commitment to excellence, innovation, and continuous growth drives our success. As we expand, we are seeking enthusiastic and responsible freshers to join our team as Process Associates.

Position Overview

The Process Associate (Fresher) will support employee attendance tracking, workplace monitoring, and operational documentation. This is an entry-level role suitable for candidates who are willing to learn, follow company policies, and work closely with HR, IT, and Security teams.

Key Responsibilities

Attendance Monitoring


Assist in tracking daily employee attendance
Support verification of punctuality, absences, and late arrivals
Help generate daily, weekly, and monthly attendance reports
Share reports with HR and management teams


CCTV & Surveillance Support


Assist in monitoring CCTV systems to ensure workplace safety
Report suspicious activities or issues to the concerned team
Maintain basic logs of surveillance recordings


Login/Logout Monitoring


Support tracking of employee login and logout times
Maintain shift-wise working hour records
Identify basic discrepancies and report them to HR


Alarm & Access Control Support


Assist in maintaining alarm system codes and access permissions
Update access lists under supervision
Follow security procedures as per company policy


Operational Support


Assist HR and Operations teams with attendance-related tasks
Maintain records related to attendance and employee logs
Support onboarding activities such as system access setup
Assist during audits when required


Reporting & Documentation


Maintain organized records of attendance and login/logout data
Prepare reports under supervision
Provide required documents during internal reviews


Support & Coordination


Coordinate with HR, IT, and Security teams as needed
Provide support to employees regarding attendance queries
Follow instructions from supervisors and management


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in any discipline (Mandatory)
Freshers are welcome to apply
Basic computer knowledge (MS Excel, MS Word)
Good communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Willingness to learn and work in night shifts
High level of integrity and confidentiality


Diversity, Equality, and Inclusion

Diversity, equality, and inclusion are fundamental to our success at HUMS. We actively promote diversity across all aspects of our organization and foster an inclusive culture where everyone has equal opportunities to grow and succeed.

Work Schedule & Benefits


Competitive salary (including EPF and ESIC)
Health insurance
Four-day work week (Monday – Thursday)
Opportunities for career growth and professional development
Food and cab-drop facilities available

Work Mode: Work From Office only


Application Process

title: Payer Support Specialist
description:
About InvoiceCloud

InvoiceCloud is a fast-growing fintech leader recognized with 20 major awards in 2025, including USA TODAY and Boston Globe Top Workplaces, multiple SaaS Awards wins for Best Solution for Finance and FinTech, and national customer service honors from Stevie and the Business Intelligence Group. Judges also highlighted our mission to reduce digital exclusion and restore simplicity and dignity to how people pay for essential services, as well as our leadership in AI maturity and responsible innovation. It’s an award-winning, purpose-driven environment where top talent thrives. To learn more, visit InvoiceCloud.com.

Job Summary

Payer Support Specialists will address support needs from end users / payers for our customers (billers.) In this role, you will use your passion for technology-fueled business solutions to provide our customers with a world-class experience. We are seeking entry-level, customer-focused individuals who are passionate about growing with our company. The role offers an opportunity to learn about the Bill Presentment and Payment space (EBPP) within the SaaS industry. Recent college graduates are encouraged to apply. .

Responsibilities


Answer phone calls and respond to tickets from payers
Have a high-level understanding of how the InvoiceCloud platform works for billers. This allows the PSS to understand issues, describe them, and offer resolutions to Payers via phone or email.
Be well versed on all Invoice Cloud products to be able to document or escalate issues to a biller.
Leverage PSN’s Agent Connect Portal to facilitate payments.
Have a high-level understanding of how the InvoiceCloud platform works for billers. This allows the PSS to understand issues, describe them, and offer resolutions to Payers via phone or email.
Document or escalate issues to the Manager, when needed


Work Arrangement & Shift Details


Work From Office (WFO): This role is based in the Hyderabad office
Shift Timing: US Eastern Time business hours, which fall in the evening to night hours IST
Candidates must be comfortable working night shifts on a regular basis
Transportation: InvoiceCloud provides two-way transportation for employees working night shifts


Goals


First 30 Days
Gain a complete understanding of the IC solutions
Gain a complete understanding of the IC internal systems including the CRM, ITR’s, Helpdesk.
Attend training, review all material provided and complete quizzes administered.
Sit with the Payer Support team to shadow them and learn from experience how to handle calls and emails.



First 60 days
Be aware of tax billing cycles as well as utilities to handle calls according to business’ natural spikes
Start solving tickets from Payers – sent via email
Start taking calls from Payer on the assigned schedule
Document all calls in the ticketing system
To have calls duration of 7 minutes (average) or less
First 90 Days and Beyond
Resolve payer calls (at least 90%) without supervisor assistance
Solve all issues involving fees when amounts are <$500 without escalation
Solve at least 90% of the calls with one touch
Solve all tickets (closed) – average 6 hours
Maintain a monthly average of 6 business hours for ticket resolution
Maintain a monthly average for call duration equal or less than 6 minutes
Complete the Internal Certification Program


What We Seek


Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Ability to learn and become well-versed with InvoiceCloud’s products and services
Customer centric, takes ownership, and results-driven
Good interpersonal skills and attention to detail
Self-starter with a demonstrated ability to achieve results as part of a high-performing team, and ability to effectively prioritize and multi-task under deadlines.
Excellent time management, organizational and planning skills


Education And Experience


Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience
Experience using standard MS Office tools
Salesforce (not required)


Payment Processing At a SAAS Company (not Required)

InvoiceCloud is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants. We do not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind based on race, color, religion, age, sex, nationality, disability, genetic information, veteran or military status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected under applicable laws.

This commitment applies to all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leave, compensation, and training.

If you require a disability-related or religious accommodation during the application or recruitment process, and wish to discuss possible adjustments, please contact jobs@invoicecloud.com.

Click here to review InvoiceCloud’s Job Applicant Privacy Policy.

For recruitment agencies: InvoiceCloud does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to our job aliases, employees, or any other company location. InvoiceCloud is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited submissions.

title: Pre-Sales Engineer
description:
Date: Mar 27, 2026

Location:

Hyderabad, TG, IN, 500032

Functional Area: Hybrid

Syntax is a leading Managed Cloud Provider for Mission Critical Enterprise Applications and has been providing comprehensive technology solutions to businesses of all sizes since 1972. Syntax has undisputed strength to implement and manage ERP deployments (Oracle, SAP) in a secure and resilient private, public or hybrid cloud. With strong technical and functional consulting services, and world-class monitoring and automation, Syntax serves some of North America’s largest corporations across a diverse range of industries. Syntax has offices worldwide, and partners with Oracle, SAP, AWS, Microsoft, IBM and other global technology leaders.

Job Title: Entry-Level Pre-Sales Engineer

Location: Hybrid/Remote In-Office

Job Type: Full-Time | Entry-Level

About The Role

We are seeking a highly motivated Entry-Level Pre-Sales Engineer to join our growing team. This role is designed for recent graduates eager to apply their technical and analytical skills in a customer-facing environment. The Pre-Sales Engineer will support Pre-Sales Solution Architects by conducting technology research, assisting with services and cloud pricing, and performing configuration item and contract reconciliation. This position offers the unique opportunity to be mentored by the most senior technical resources in the company, providing a solid foundation for career growth in pre-sales engineering, solution architecture, and enterprise IT consulting.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct technology research to support solution design and customer requirements.
Assist in pricing and configuration of IT services and cloud solutions by analyzing vendor pricing models and cost structures.
Support cloud pricing analysis and configuration for customer proposals.
Perform configuration item and contract reconciliation to ensure accuracy in customer agreements and solution designs.
Collaborate with Solution Architects to build technology solutions that align with customer needs.
Develop a strong understanding of enterprise computing concepts, including ERP, databases, networking, operating systems, data centers, and cloud computing.
Prepare and maintain documentation related to solution designs, pricing, and configurations.
Work with cross-functional teams, including sales, operations, and finance, to ensure accurate and competitive solutions.
Participate in training and mentorship programs led by senior engineers and solution architects.


Required Qualifications & Skills


Bachelor’s degree in Management Information Systems (MIS), Computer Science, Accounting, Finance, or a related business function with relevant computer science coursework.
Strong analytical and research skills with the ability to interpret and present technical information.
Basic knowledge of enterprise IT environments, including ERP systems, networking, operating systems, databases, data centers, and cloud computing platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud).
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other financial or pricing analysis tools.
Ability to learn quickly and adapt to new technologies and business models.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
No prior work experience required; internships or academic projects related to enterprise IT, cloud computing, or technology research are a plus.


What We Offer


Hands-on mentorship from senior technical leaders.
Exposure to cutting-edge enterprise technologies and cloud solutions.
A structured career development path in pre-sales engineering and solution architecture.
A dynamic and supportive work environment with opportunities to engage in real-world IT solution development.
Competitive salary and benefits package.


If you are a recent graduate with a passion for technology, enterprise IT, and business solutions, we encourage you to apply and start your journey with us!

Why Syntax?

Become a part of our success story and work in a company with exciting innovation projects that are causing a stir across the industry. We recently launched one of the world's most advanced manufacturing facilities based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud for Execution - for Smart Press Shop, a pioneering joint venture between Porsche and forming specialist Schuler.


Competitive, above-average compensation
Global tourist: With us, you can also work from abroad from time to time
Flexible working time models, home office
Attractive benefits, e.g. company pension scheme or various health offers
A modern environment in which the "you" is part of it
Open feedback culture, flat hierarchies and a motivated team
Individual career planning with continuous training and coaching on the job


You see a personal challenge in this responsible task? Apply now - and become part of the SYNTAX team!

title: Media Analytics Associate
description:
Level: Associate (L3)

Role: Marketing Investment Optimization – Media Analytics

Mio Overview

The Marketing Investment Optimization (MIO) vertical at Amgen is responsible for enabling data‑driven, decision‑grade marketing investment decisions across brands, channels, and portfolios.

MIO brings together Investment Analytics (IA), Media Analytics (MA), and Marketing Science Operations (MSO) to measure effectiveness, quantify incrementality, and optimize marketing investments.

MIO focuses on marketing mix modelling (MMx), Experimentation and Causal Measurement, Optimization, Media and Campaign analytics, Scenario Planning, ensuring that marketing dollars are allocated to maximize business impact. The team partners closely with Brand Marketing, GCC/Agencies, CD&A, and MSO to translate complex analytics into actionable investment decisions.

Role Summary

The Associate – Media Analytics role is an entry‑level analytics position within the Media Analytics team. This role is ideal for candidate who are excited about applying analytical thinking, structured problem solving, and data skills to real‑world business questions.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an Associate, you will work under guidance from Senior Associates and Managers to support analytical deliverables and build strong foundations in applied analytics.

You will:


Support campaign and media performance reporting across TV, digital, and other paid media channels
Assist in data extraction, cleaning, validation, and reconciliation from multiple media data sources
Build and maintain tables, charts, and dashboards to track key media KPIs
Perform quality checks (QA) on media metrics to ensure accuracy and consistency
Help analyze trends in delivery, reach, frequency, engagement, and outcome proxies
Assist in preparing weekly and monthly readouts, summaries, and presentation materials
Collaborate with team members to meet project timelines and quality standards and actively communicate project status updates.
Engage in ongoing learning to develop industry knowledge, and technical capabilities.


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's (3 to 6 Yrs) or master's (2 to 4 Yrs) degree required in any discipline with strong record of academic success in quantitative and analytic coursework such as operations research, applied mathematics, management science, data science, statistics, econometrics or engineering.
Experience in any analytical, data, or problem‑solving role.
Experience with Excel, SQL, or any data analysis tool.


Preferred Qualifications


Demonstrated ability to perform structured QA and validation.
Familiarity with dashboards, KPIs, or reporting; Interest in media, digital marketing, or performance measurement
Experience supporting analytics in pharma, healthcare, or regulated environments.


Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate closely with Senior Associates to deliver high‑quality analytical outputs.

title: Executive - Talent Acquisition
description:
Job Description

About KPMG in India

KPMG entities in India are professional services firm(s). These Indian member firms are affiliated with KPMG International Limited. KPMG was established in India in August 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, and are conversant with local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG has offices across India in Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chandigarh, Chennai, Gurugram, Jaipur, Hyderabad, Jaipur, Kochi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Noida, Pune, Vadodara and Vijayawada.

KPMG entities in India offer services to national and international clients in India across sectors. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment.

Overview

KPMG entities in India are professional services firm(s). Member firms are affiliated with KPMG International Limited. KPMG was established in India in August 1993. KPMG entities in India offer services to and international clients in India across sectors. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the business environment.

Why Choose KPMG India?


Dynamic Work Environment : Be a part of an innovative team that values creativity and fresh ideas.
Growth Opportunities : We provide excellent training, mentorship, and career advancement.
Attractive Benefits : Competitive salaries, health insurance, and more!



Role Summary

The Junior Recruiter will support KPMG’s Talent Acquisition team in executing end-to-end recruitment activities for entry-level to mid-level roles. This role focuses on sourcing, screening, coordinating interviews, and delivering a positive candidate experience while ensuring compliance with KPMG’s recruitment policies and standards.

Key Responsibilities Recruitment & Sourcing

title: KYC Operations Analyst 1
description:
The KYC Operations Analyst 1 is an entry-level position responsible for participating in Anti-Money Laundering (AML) monitoring, governance, oversight and regulatory reporting activities in coordination with the Compliance and Control team. The overall objective of this role is to assist in the development and management of a dedicated internal KYC (Know Your Client) program at Citi.

Responsibilities:


Partner with Relationship Management and Compliance teams to assist with the preparation, development, due diligence and approval of the electronic Know Your Client (KYC) record and supporting appendices
Create and maintain KYC records by obtaining information from internal and external sources (firm website, regulatory websites, etc.)
Partner with Relationship Management and Compliance teams to update system information from initiation to approval of KYC record and report workflow progress to supervisor
Validate the information within KYC records and Customer Identification Program (CIP) documents to ensure completion and accuracy
Ensure KYC records incorporate local regulatory requirements / Global Business Support Unit (BSU) Standards
Maintain BSU tool
Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.


Knowledge/Experience:


2-3 years of work experience with basic understanding of relevant banking practices, corporate policies, and compliance requirements.
Good command of reading and writing English.
Good PC skills with ability to pick up new software systems.
Co-operative, proactive with high sense of responsibility & teamwork
Ability to work under pressure and in a volatile environment
Attention to details with high accuracy in work; Strive for process improvement.
Display the ability to prioritize effectively to meet routine processing deadlines
Flexible enough to work as per Business timings.
ACAMS certified (an advantage)


Qualifications:


University degree preferably in Business/Finance, Math, Economics, Engineering, Optimization, or Law.


Skills:


Proven ability to work independently and be self-starter
Exceptional organization and process management ability
Intermediate technical skills including intermediate working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.)
Demonstrated ability to prioritize, multi-task and work within tight and changeable timeframe while still maintaining high level of accuracy
Strong analytical skills and time management skills, excellent attention to details
Excellent written and verbal communication skills and aptitude in communicating with senior management
Ability to work with internal stakeholders (e.g. Coverage, Quality Assurance, Compliance) & partners (e.g. Training, Communications, Senior Management) to develop and execute on process enhancements and simplifications
Ability to work well under pressure and tight time frames
Pro-active, flexible, have good organizational skills and must be team player.
Ability to work in a dynamic environment


------------------------------------------------------

Job Family Group:

Operations - Services

------------------------------------------------------

Job Family:

Business KYC

------------------------------------------------------

Full time

------------------------------------------------------

Most Relevant Skills

Business Acumen, Credible Challenge, Laws and Regulations, Management Reporting, Policy and Procedure, Program Management, Referral and Escalation, Risk Controls and Monitors, Risk Identification and Assessment, Risk Remediation.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description:
We invite you to come grow with us.

Inizio Ignite is the global advisory partner for health and life sciences, encompassing connected expertise across Research Partnership, Putnam, Vynamic, and STEM. Guided by purpose, passion, and precision, we collaborate with clients to ignite impact, accelerate performance, and deliver lasting change for patients. By uniting strategy, insights, and innovation with a unique depth and breadth of expertise, we drive transformation and shape the future of health.

Our Research Partnership healthcare market research team provides research and insights to inform and power healthcare decisions. Serving a variety of clients across the lifecycle, our expertise includes data, research & insights (proprietary syndicated and custom research) and brand tracking (ATU). Proudly part of Inizio – the market-leading commercialization partner for health and life sciences. From early-stage development to loss of exclusivity, we simplify complexity, unlock value, and make collaboration seamless at every pivotal moment – supporting confident, insight-driven decisions that move innovation forward.

About the role:

The Data Quality Analyst role will work alongside colleagues in Europe, India and Singapore on major Brand Equity tracking projects for a global client covering multiple Therapy Areas across a number of international markets. The position will report into the Program Data Manager, based in the UK, with direct colleagues in analytics based in India and the UK. This is a high profile client and project for our business, providing a platform for the project team to have a significant impact on the client’s brand strategies.

The role will involve working with our Research project teams, the Data Management & Dashboards team and with analytics experts to investigate and check data across multiple workstreams for our major tracking programs, primarily using Python.

About the team:

The Gurugram hub office is part of our global team, partnering with colleagues across different functional areas and specialisms. This is an exciting period of growth for Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership, as we expand into the fast-growing Indian market.

Your primary role:

As Data Quality Analyst, you will support the Program Data Manager to ensure data quality and consistency throughout the reporting cycle on our Major Tracking programs. You will also support with advising the Research project teams and Data Management & Dashboard teams based in Europe, India and Singapore.

Technical Expertise


Python (3 to 6 years) experience for data processing, automation, and pipeline support
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation, storage, outputs)


Database Expertise


Strong SQL skills using PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience with data modelling and working with data marts / warehouses to support dashboard performance.


Dashboarding & Visualization


Hands-on experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools
Ability to support dashboard population, maintenance, and data validation


Cloud & Platform Exposure (Nice to Have)


Exposure to cloud-based data processing environments (AWS or Google Cloud)
Familiarity with version control and structured deployment processes


Soft Skills & Behaviors


Clear communicator with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Strong ownership and accountability for end-to-end data delivery
High attention to detail with a strong data quality mindset
Ability to prioritise and manage multiple tasks in a deadline-driven environment
Strong analytical, problem-solving skills and Comfortable working in global, cross-functional teams


Life at Inizio Ignite | Research Partnership:

We love what we do and continuously strive for innovation and creativity. We believe everyone should continuously be learning and provide:


A comprehensive training and development programme for all staff, from entry level apprentice to senior management
Opportunities for international travel and collaboration
A relaxed and friendly working environment


Further information:

This is a full-time role and is available in a hybrid pattern, based at our fantastic Inizio Advisory location in Gurugram. This role’s working hours will be 13:30 to 22:30 from Monday to Thursday and 11:30 to 20:30 on Friday.

Any specific information that you would want us to gather from the candidates.

Technical:


Experience with Python
Experience with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MS SQL
Experience managing end-to-end data pipelines
Experience with data warehousing / data marts


Capabilities:


Ability to work autonomously with limited direct oversight / instruction
Client-facing capabilities to explain data management challenges/requirements
Working with a globally distributed team


Don't meet every job requirement? That's okay! Our company is dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you're excited about this role, but your experience doesn't perfectly fit every qualification, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this role or others.

title: Associate Data Analyst, Power BI & SQL
description:
Owens & Minor is a global healthcare solutions company providing essential products, services and technology solutions that support care delivery in leading hospitals, health systems and research centers around the world. For over 140 years, Owens & Minor has delivered comfort and confidence behind the scenes, so healthcare stays at the forefront, helping to make each day better for the hospitals, healthcare partners, and communities we serve. Powered by more than 14,000 teammates worldwide, Owens & Minor exists because every day, everywhere, Life Takes Care™.

Global Reach with a Local Touch

140+ years serving healthcare
Over 14,000 teammates worldwide
Serving healthcare partners in 80 countries
Manufacturing facilities in the U.S., Honduras, Mexico, Thailand and Ireland
40+ distribution centers
Portfolio of 300 propriety and branded product offerings
1,000 branded medical product suppliers
4,000 healthcare partners served




Position Overview:

We are seeking a motivated and enthusiastic Entry-Level Power BI Developer/Support Person to join our team. In this role, you will have the opportunity to learn and grow your skills in developing and supporting Power BI solutions. You will work closely with experienced professionals, collaborate on data visualization projects, and provide technical support to end users. This position offers an excellent entry point into the world of business intelligence and data analytics.




Responsibilities:

Assist in the development and maintenance of interactive dashboards, reports, and data visualizations using Power BI.
Collaborate with business stakeholders and senior developers to understand requirements and contribute to the design and development of effective data visualizations.
Support the creation and management of data models, including DAX calculations and measures, to enable business analytics.
Assist in optimizing Power BI solutions for performance, usability, and scalability.
Provide technical support and troubleshooting to end users, addressing report creation, data connectivity, and functionality questions.
Contribute to the implementation of data governance practices, ensuring data integrity, security, and compliance.
Collaborate with the team to document data visualization processes, procedures, and best practices.
Learn and stay updated with the latest Power BI features, capabilities, and industry trends.
Support data validation and quality assurance activities, ensuring accurate and reliable reporting outputs.
Take part in training and development activities to enhance Power BI skills and expertise.




Required Skills and Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, or a related field. Relevant certifications or coursework in data analytics are a plus.
Basic understanding of data visualization concepts and the importance of data-driven decision-making.
Familiarity (two years) with Power BI or other business intelligence tools is preferred but not mandatory.
Proficiency in SQL and experience with data querying and manipulation.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with an eagerness to learn and develop new technical skills.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment.
Basic understanding of data governance, data quality, and security principles.
Attention to detail and the ability to manage tasks effectively and meet deadlines.




Preferred Qualifications:

Experience or coursework in data modeling, database design, or data warehousing concepts.
Familiarity with Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel.
Knowledge of data integration and ETL processes.
Understanding of relational databases and data management principles.




If you are an entry-level professional with a passion for data visualization, eager to learn and grow your skills in Power BI development and support, we encourage you to apply for this position. Join our team and kick-start your career in business intelligence and data analytics!




If you feel this opportunity could be the next step in your career, we encourage you to apply. This position will accept applications on an ongoing basis.

Owens & Minor is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, genetic information, religion, disability, age, status as a veteran, or any other status prohibited by applicable national, federal, state or local law.

title: Seller Operations Executive
description:
About noon

We’re building an ecosystem of digital products and services that power everyday life across the Middle East—fast, scalable, and deeply customer-centric. Our mission is to deliver to every door every day. We want to redefine what technology can do in this region, and we’re looking for a Seller Operator (Operations) who can help us move even faster.




Our mission: Ring every doorbell, every day.




What you'll do:

At noon, you’ll work alongside some of the fastest, smartest, and most driven people in the region. Our teams are young, ambitious, and relentlessly execution-focused, working on high-impact initiatives every day.




Overview of the role:

Looking for a dynamic Operations Associate to support forward logistics, reverse operations, and seller onboarding. The role involves managing order flows, handling returns, and assisting sellers to ensure smooth marketplace operations. Ideal for candidates who are detail-oriented, eager to learn, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.




What you'll need:

1. Forward Operations:

Assist in the day-to-day execution of forward logistics operations.
Support order processing activities to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
Collaborate with team members to maintain optimal inventory levels and smooth shipment processes.
Learn and contribute to the optimization of supply chain operations.




2. Reverse Operations:

Support the management of the reverse logistics process, including returns and exchanges.
Assist in the implementation of effective return policies to minimize costs and enhance customer satisfaction.
Analyze return trends under guidance to provide insights for process improvement.
Work closely with the team to address and resolve return-related inquiries.




3. Seller Onboarding:

Contribute to the onboarding process for new sellers onto our platform.
Assist in maintaining up-to-date onboarding documentation for sellers.
Provide support in training sessions for sellers and act as a point of contact for basic inquiries.
Collaborate with team members to streamline and enhance the overall onboarding process.




Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration or equivalent practical experience.
Fluent in English with excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Entry-level candidates or 1-3 years of relevant work experience. -Strong organizational skills with attention to detail. -Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment. -Eagerness to learn and contribute to process improvement.




Who will excel?

We’re looking for candidates who thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic start-up environment.
We’re searching for problem solvers, people who operate with a bias for action and have a deep understanding of the importance of resourcefulness over reliance.
Candor is our only default. Demanding unequivocal high standards should be non-negotiable because quality matters.
We want people who are radically candid, cohorts who commit to settling for nothing but the best - in hiring, in accepting work from colleagues, and in your own work. Ours is not an easy mission, but it is a meaningful one.
Every hire must actively raise the bar of talent in the company to help us reach our vision.
